title: Physics Awareness in 3D Gaussian Splatting: Simulation, Identification, and Physical Image Formation
scope: Strict Gaussian-splatting-centric mechanics, inverse identification and digital twins, learned physics/control, and physics-based image formation; first-public work families through the cutoff

search_method.workflow: Firecrawl research-papers discovery followed by primary arXiv, DOI, and publisher-record verification
query_families: 3D Gaussian Splatting physics simulation, MPM, FEM, PBD, SPH, spring-mass, mechanics
Gaussian Splatting deformable simulation, cloth, tissue, soft body, contact, collision
Gaussian Splatting fluid, granular material, fracture, thermomechanics
Gaussian Splatting system identification, inverse dynamics, stiffness, friction, density, force
Gaussian Splatting learned physics, world model, digital twin, robotics, intervention, control
3D Gaussian Splatting inverse rendering, BRDF, materials, lighting, relighting, shadows
Gaussian Splatting reflection, specular transport, global illumination, ray/path tracing
Gaussian Splatting transparency, refraction, glass, water, caustics, transmission
Gaussian Splatting participating media, scattering, underwater, spectral, thermal, radiative imaging
Gaussian Splatting camera formation, distortion, rolling shutter, blur, defocus, event sensors
search_method.inclusion: A technical work with a novel method/evaluation in which Gaussian splatting is structurally central to the physical state, inverse observation model, learned physical state, or image/sensor formation model
search_method.exclusion: Generic dynamic reconstruction with only kinematic regularization; non-Gaussian roots; surveys, blogs, and vendor claims; generic enhancement where the Gaussian renderer is incidental
search_method.deduplication: Preprint and venue versions merged as one work family; first-public year retained; titles and stable primary URLs normalized

notes: Access-neutral novelty screen: publisher-only and paywalled records are eligible, but no paywall was bypassed.
E1 records support novelty/metadata only; detailed performance, metric, capability, and hardware figures use E2/E3 full-text records.
Novelty and hardware are independent axes. Hardware is transcribed only when recovered from primary evidence; no consumer-feasibility inference is made.
Counts describe this reproducible strict-scope corpus, not all publications worldwide. The 2026 count is partial through 20 July.

research_agenda.label: Joint visual–physical benchmark
research_agenda.rationale: Current benchmarks isolate appearance, mechanics, optics, or control; none provides a common accounting boundary for geometry, parameters, interventions, contacts, and compute.

research_agenda.label: Uncertainty-aware system identification
research_agenda.rationale: Most inverse pipelines return one parameter vector even when geometry, excitation, force, lighting, and material variables are not uniquely observable.

research_agenda.label: Contact and topology validation
research_agenda.rationale: Collision, fracture, cutting, and tearing are common demonstrations, but measured contact timing, penetration, impulses, and topology consistency remain rare.

research_agenda.label: Physics-state compression
research_agenda.rationale: Existing physics-aware assets report compute but do not jointly rate–distortion code velocity, stress, material parameters, contacts, uncertainty, and renderable appearance.

research_agenda.label: Online optomechanical digital twin
research_agenda.rationale: Mechanical identification typically assumes a sufficient renderer while optical inverse rendering assumes a static scene; joint online observability and latency are unresolved.

research_agenda.label: Calibrated secondary light transport
research_agenda.rationale: Reflection, refraction, and global-illumination methods improve images, but controlled ground truth, uncertainty, and transport-specific cost accounting are inconsistent.

research_agenda.label: Sensor-aware physics under adverse capture
research_agenda.rationale: Underwater, thermal, radiative, event, and nonlinear-camera models are advancing separately from mechanical identification and intervention evidence.

papers.title: i-PhysGaussian: Implicit Physical Simulation for 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Used an implicit MPM integrator for stiff and quasi-static Gaussian assets that are fragile under explicit small-step updates.
papers.gaussian_role: appearance and MPM particles
papers.physical_model: Implicit MPM
papers.inputs: Static Gaussian assets and prescribed loads/materials
papers.evaluation: Accuracy/stability and runtime comparisons
papers.hardware: RTX 4090 24 GB
papers.limitations: Preprint; forward parameters remain supplied.

papers.title: D-REX: Differentiable Real-to-Sim-to-Real Engine for Learning Dexterous Grasping
papers.novelty: Used Gaussian/2DGS digital twins and differentiable rigid dynamics to identify mass before force-aware grasp-policy learning.
papers.gaussian_role: visual observation and digital-twin appearance
papers.physical_model: Differentiable rigid-body dynamics
papers.inputs: Object scans and robot control signals
papers.evaluation: Mass recovery and dexterous grasp success
papers.hardware: RTX 4090; reconstruction 30–35 min/object, identification 5–20 min, inference about 0.5 s
papers.limitations: Mass-focused offline system with difficulty on tiny objects.

papers.title: GaussTwin: Unified Simulation and Correction with Gaussian Splatting for Robotic Digital Twins
papers.novelty: Anchored Gaussians to PBD/Cosserat elements and corrected the digital twin online at pose level.
papers.gaussian_role: appearance rigidly anchored to physical elements
papers.physical_model: PBD plus discrete Cosserat rods
papers.inputs: Four D415 cameras for initialization and three for tracking
papers.evaluation: Tracking, correction, simulation and manipulation
papers.hardware: RTX 4090; 25 Hz total about 40 ms: segmentation 24 ms, pose 10 ms, simulation/I/O 6 ms
papers.limitations: Calibrated lab setting and known object abstraction.

papers.title: DiffWind: Physics-Informed Differentiable Modeling of Wind-Driven Object Dynamics
papers.novelty: Jointly fitted an invisible grid wind field and deformable Gaussian object through differentiable LBM–MPM–rendering.
papers.gaussian_role: appearance observation and physical object particles
papers.physical_model: Coupled LBM wind and MPM object dynamics
papers.inputs: Monocular GoPro video with depth/MLLM initialization
papers.evaluation: Flow, geometry, rollout and wind/material tests
papers.hardware: RTX 4090 24 GB; about 2 h/scene; 1.17 s/optimization step; 12.3–21 GB across grids
papers.limitations: Monocular ambiguity and expensive per-scene optimization.

papers.title: UAV-Assisted Scan-to-Simulation for Landslides Using Physics-Informed Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Fed UAV Gaussian terrain reconstruction into MPM landslide particles and mapped simulated motion back to splats.
papers.gaussian_role: terrain observation and simulation visualization
papers.physical_model: MPM landslide model
papers.inputs: UAV imagery and reconstructed terrain
papers.evaluation: Preliminary terrain/simulation demonstrations
papers.hardware: RTX 4090; runtime NR; UAV sensor setup
papers.limitations: Simplified geomechanics and limited quantitative validation.

papers.title: PG-3DGS: Optimizing 3D Gaussian Splatting to Satisfy Physics Objectives
papers.novelty: Backpropagated differentiable fluid objectives into Gaussian geometry so assets satisfy functional flow goals as well as images.
papers.gaussian_role: design variables and renderer
papers.physical_model: Differentiable fluid simulation
papers.inputs: Gaussian asset, images and target functional objective
papers.evaluation: Image/geometry quality and physical function success
papers.hardware: NR
papers.limitations: Task-conditioned inverse design can move geometry away from real shape.

papers.title: Real2Sim: A Physics-Driven and Editable Gaussian Splatting Framework for Autonomous Driving Scenes
papers.novelty: Made object Gaussians carry MPM mass, volume, velocity and deformation state in editable driving reconstructions.
papers.gaussian_role: object appearance and MPM particles
papers.physical_model: MPM object dynamics
papers.inputs: Waymo five-camera, 10 Hz capture
papers.evaluation: Driving reconstruction, edit and simulation quality
papers.hardware: RTX 4090 24 GB; 50k StreetGaussian iterations; five cameras at 10 Hz
papers.limitations: Unseen sides and decoupled simulation/rendering remain weak.

papers.title: EndoGSim: Physics-Aware 4D Dynamic Endoscopic Scene Simulations via MLLM-Guided Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Initialized tissue materials with an MLLM and refined them using render/flow losses through differentiable MLS-MPM.
papers.gaussian_role: dynamic renderer and tissue particles
papers.physical_model: Differentiable MLS-MPM
papers.inputs: Endoscopic video with flow and material priors
papers.evaluation: Reconstruction, flow, material and simulation tests
papers.hardware: A6000; 50^3 grid and 25 substeps per rendered frame
papers.limitations: Material ground truth is largely simulated and endoscopic observations remain ill posed.

papers.title: MonoPhysics: Estimating Geometry, Appearance, and Physical Parameters from Monocular Videos
papers.novelty: Jointly optimized monocular geometry, scale, appearance and deformable material parameters through differentiable MPM and GS.
papers.gaussian_role: appearance observation and physical particles
papers.physical_model: Differentiable MPM
papers.inputs: Single-camera deforming-object video
papers.evaluation: Geometry, appearance, parameter and rollout error
papers.hardware: RTX A6000; about 2 h/scene; 200–250 physics iterations plus 5k appearance steps
papers.limitations: Scale/material ambiguity and per-scene cost remain fundamental.

papers.title: Learning Action-Conditional and Object-Centric Gaussian Splatting World Models for Rigid Objects (MRO-GWM)
papers.novelty: Represented multiple objects with Gaussian rigid states and learned action-conditioned prediction for MPC.
papers.gaussian_role: object-centric visual and rigid state
papers.physical_model: Action-conditioned transformer over rigid states
papers.inputs: Simulated robot manipulation sequences
papers.evaluation: Prediction, rendering and MPC success
papers.hardware: A40 training about 22 h/60k steps; model 5 Hz, simulator 100 Hz
papers.limitations: Rigid-only and simulation-trained.

papers.title: PersistGS: Differentiable Physics for Object Permanence in 4D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Fitted friction and velocity with differentiable rigid physics so Gaussian objects persist through complete multiview occlusion.
papers.gaussian_role: dynamic appearance plus rigid state
papers.physical_model: Differentiable rigid-body contact
papers.inputs: Five training and two evaluation cameras, 512² at 60 FPS
papers.evaluation: Occluded trajectory, rendering and parameter tests
papers.hardware: RTX 5080 16 GB; fitting about 5.5 min/scene
papers.limitations: Only three synthetic ball/contact scenes; mass and contact stiffness fixed.

papers.title: Scene-Level Heterogeneous Physics Simulation with 3D Gaussian Splats
papers.novelty: Coupled captured splats, meshes, rigid bodies and deformables within one scene kernel and recoupled results for UE5 rendering.
papers.gaussian_role: scene appearance and cross-representation interface
papers.physical_model: Heterogeneous rigid/deformable solvers
papers.inputs: Captured scenes and manually prepared assets
papers.evaluation: Five heterogeneous interaction sequences and stage time
papers.hardware: Standard workstation; all five sequences under 10 min; explicitly offline
papers.limitations: Manual UE5 preparation and no end-to-end online rate.

papers.title: MeGAS: Thermomechanical Dynamic Gaussian Splatting for Thermophysical Scene Editing
papers.novelty: Added temperature and phase-change state to dynamic Gaussians with coupled thermal and mechanical solvers.
papers.gaussian_role: appearance and thermomechanical material state
papers.physical_model: Coupled thermal/mechanical phase-change simulation
papers.inputs: Synthetic assets and iPhone captures
papers.evaluation: Rendering, phase evolution and qualitative thermophysical tests
papers.hardware: iPhone capture; compute/runtime NR
papers.limitations: Preprint with synthetic-heavy material validation.

papers.title: Occlusion-Robust Multi-Object Decoupling for Physics-Based Robotic Interaction
papers.novelty: Used joint 2D/3D priors to extract complete Gaussian object assets for collision-aware MPM robot interaction.
papers.gaussian_role: object appearance and simulation regions
papers.physical_model: MPM interaction after object completion
papers.inputs: Occluded multiview scenes and robot targets
papers.evaluation: Image metrics and qualitative interactions
papers.hardware: NR
papers.limitations: Physics is evaluated mainly through images and qualitative contact.

papers.title: FluidGS: Physics-Informed Gaussian Splatting for Dynamic Fluid Reconstruction from Sparse Views
papers.novelty: Introduced physics-informed sparse-view dynamic fluid reconstruction with Gaussian primitives.
papers.gaussian_role: dynamic fluid appearance and constrained state
papers.physical_model: Physics-informed fluid constraints
papers.inputs: Sparse multiview fluid observations
papers.evaluation: Dynamic reconstruction and physical-consistency evidence
papers.hardware: NR in accessible primary abstract
papers.limitations: Publisher evidence supports novelty, not detailed implementation or hardware claims.

papers.title: Relightable 3D Gaussians: Realistic Point Cloud Relighting with BRDF Decomposition and Ray Tracing
papers.novelty: Associates normals, BRDF parameters, and directional incident illumination with splats, then adds BVH point-based ray tracing for visibility and shadows.
papers.gaussian_role: The Gaussian point cloud is the primary optimizable geometry and material carrier.
papers.physical_model: Microfacet-style BRDF decomposition plus the rendering equation and ray-traced visibility.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images under fixed unknown illumination.
papers.evaluation: Novel-view, normal, BRDF, and relighting comparisons using PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS and material-error measures.
papers.hardware: GPU model NR in the inspected paper; about 0.90 h optimization per scene is reported.
papers.limitations: Relighting inherits geometry/normal errors; visibility is precomputed and the capture assumptions do not identify fully general spatially varying transport.

papers.title: GS-IR: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Derives normals from Gaussian depth and bakes occlusion to recover geometry, materials, and environment illumination with forward splatting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians jointly carry radiance, normals, and PBR material parameters.
papers.physical_model: Physically based surface shading with environment illumination, normal regularization, and baked visibility/indirect-light approximations.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images captured under one unknown illumination.
papers.evaluation: Novel view synthesis, normal/albedo/roughness recovery, and relighting on synthetic and real inverse-rendering scenes.
papers.hardware: GPU model NR in the inspected paper; roughly 45 min optimization per scene is reported.
papers.limitations: Baked occlusion is not a full differentiable multi-bounce transport solution and the inverse problem remains illumination-material ambiguous.

papers.title: GaussianShader: 3D Gaussian Splatting with Shading Functions for Reflective Surfaces
papers.novelty: Introduces a compact shading function and a shortest-axis normal-consistency scheme to model specular surfaces without abandoning splat rasterization.
papers.gaussian_role: Each Gaussian is treated as a local oriented surface sample with learned shading parameters.
papers.physical_model: Simplified physically motivated diffuse/specular shading driven by estimated normals and view direction.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images of reflective scenes.
papers.evaluation: Reflective novel-view synthesis, geometry/normal ablations, PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS, optimization time, and rendering speed.
papers.hardware: GPU model NR in the primary arXiv record; 0.58 h optimization and 97 FPS are reported.
papers.limitations: The shading model is deliberately simplified and does not explicitly solve scene lighting, global visibility, or general inter-reflection.

papers.title: GIR: 3D Gaussian Inverse Rendering for Relightable Scene Factorization
papers.novelty: Factorizes 3DGS appearance into material, illumination, and geometry terms and provides an efficient relightable asset pipeline.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are the scene representation and store factorized intrinsic attributes.
papers.physical_model: Surface rendering equation with learned material and lighting factors plus visibility approximations.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Novel views, intrinsic decomposition, normal/material recovery, relighting, and runtime.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA Tesla V100 is reported; 92.58 FPS for about 350k Gaussians. An Unreal Engine demonstration reports RTX 3090 and 78.75 FPS.
papers.limitations: Factorization is underconstrained from fixed-light RGB and approximate transport can bake residual illumination into materials.

papers.title: DeferredGS: Decoupled and Editable Gaussian Splatting with Deferred Shading
papers.novelty: Moves material and lighting evaluation to a deferred image-space stage, separating geometry/material attributes from illumination for editing and relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians rasterize a geometry/material G-buffer consumed by a deferred shading model.
papers.physical_model: PBR deferred shading with environment illumination and learned visibility/indirect components.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Novel views, normal/material decomposition, relighting and editing, PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS, training and FPS.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA RTX 3090 24 GB; 3–4 h training and about 30 FPS at 800×800 are reported.
papers.limitations: Image-space deferred shading can lose primitive-level transport detail and relies on approximate learned indirect effects.

papers.title: 3D Gaussian Splatting with Deferred Reflection
papers.access: open arXiv preprint plus publisher record
papers.novelty: Introduces a deferred reflection formulation that separates base radiance from view-dependent reflective appearance for cleaner geometry and reflection editing.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians provide a rasterized scene representation whose reflection is evaluated in a deferred stage.
papers.physical_model: Surface normals, reflection directions, and environment-conditioned specular reflection.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images containing glossy or reflective surfaces.
papers.evaluation: Reflective novel-view rendering, reflection editing, geometry/normal quality, appearance metrics, and runtime.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: The reflection branch is not a full path tracer and complex multi-bounce or transmissive transport remains outside the model.

papers.title: PRTGS: Precomputed Radiance Transfer of Gaussian Splats for Real-Time High-Quality Relighting
papers.access: open arXiv preprint plus publisher record
papers.novelty: Adapts precomputed radiance transfer to splats so complex environment-light changes can be rendered in real time.
papers.gaussian_role: Each Gaussian stores appearance and precomputed transport coefficients.
papers.physical_model: Low-frequency precomputed radiance transfer including visibility and indirect-light effects.
papers.inputs: Multi-view object images, reconstructed Gaussians, and environment lighting.
papers.evaluation: Relighting fidelity under novel environment maps, material/transport ablations, PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS, and FPS.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Precomputation targets a fixed scene and low-frequency transport; geometry or material edits generally require transport recomputation.

papers.title: 3iGS: Factorised Tensorial Illumination for 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Represents view-light-dependent appearance with a factorized tensorial illumination field, supporting novel lights without committing to a surface-normal BRDF.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry spatial features decoded with factorized view and illumination coordinates.
papers.physical_model: Data-driven factorization of the reflectance field rather than an explicitly identified BRDF.
papers.inputs: Multi-view multi-illumination imagery.
papers.evaluation: Novel view and novel-light rendering, PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS, and cost relative to baseline 3DGS.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA V100 32 GB; about 2× inference and 3.2× training slowdown relative to baseline are reported, without absolute rates in the coded evidence.
papers.limitations: Relighting is observation-driven and physical material parameters are not identified; capture requires illumination variation.

papers.title: Progressive Radiance Distillation for Inverse Rendering with Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Progressively distills complex radiance into explicit geometry, material, and illumination attributes for fast PBR relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians begin as a radiance field and become carriers of distilled surface/PBR attributes.
papers.physical_model: PBR decomposition with staged radiance-to-material distillation and visibility-aware shading.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images under fixed illumination.
papers.evaluation: Novel views, normals/materials, relighting, optimization cost, and real-time rendering.
papers.hardware: Intel i7-13700KF, 32 GB system RAM, and NVIDIA RTX 4090; 39 min plus 8 min stages and 221 FPS are reported.
papers.limitations: Teacher/distillation priors can preserve baked lighting and physical accuracy is limited by the staged decomposition assumptions.

papers.title: Subsurface Scattering for 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Combines a Gaussian surface with a volumetric radiance-transfer field to reconstruct and relight translucent objects from OLAT data.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians explicitly represent the object surface and spatially varying BRDF; an auxiliary implicit volume represents subsurface transport.
papers.physical_model: Surface BRDF, volumetric subsurface scattering, incident-light field, and differentiable ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Multi-view one-light-at-a-time images from synthetic scenes and a light-stage capture.
papers.evaluation: Novel view, material editing, novel-light relighting, and comparisons on a newly captured multi-view multi-light dataset.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Requires controlled OLAT capture and uses a hybrid non-Gaussian volume for the hardest transport component.

papers.title: SpectralGaussians: Semantic, spectral 3D Gaussian splatting for multi-spectral scene representation, visualization and analysis
papers.novelty: Extends splats to registered multispectral channels, semantic labels, and per-spectrum reflectance/lighting for cross-spectral rendering and editing.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry wavelength-indexed appearance and semantic attributes.
papers.physical_model: Spectrum-dependent reflectance and illumination with a physically based spectral shading layer.
papers.inputs: Registered multi-view multispectral images and segmentation maps.
papers.evaluation: Cross-spectral novel views, spectral reconstruction and segmentation/editing against spectral NeRF baselines.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Uses a small set of measured bands rather than continuous spectral transport, and accurate registration/calibration is assumed.

papers.title: Phys3DGS: Physically-based 3D Gaussian Splatting for Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Builds a staged physically based inverse-rendering pipeline with explicit normals, material, illumination, and Monte Carlo PBR rendering.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians encode surface geometry and PBR attributes and are directly rendered with sampled light transport.
papers.physical_model: Rendering equation with microfacet BRDF, visibility, direct and indirect illumination.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Novel views, normals/material/light decomposition, novel-light relighting and rendering speed at specified samples per pixel.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA RTX 4090; stage 1 at most 1 h, stage 2 at most 10 min, PBR optimization about 30 min, and 42.75 FPS at 64 samples per pixel.
papers.limitations: Sampled transport increases cost and recovered factors remain non-identifiable from a single unknown illumination.

papers.title: GI-GS: Global Illumination Decomposition on Gaussian Splatting for Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Separates direct and indirect illumination on splats and models occlusion to improve material-light disentanglement and relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians store geometry, BRDF parameters, and decomposed illumination components.
papers.physical_model: PBR surface shading with explicit direct/global-illumination decomposition and visibility.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Intrinsic component accuracy, relighting and NVS on TensoIR and Mip-NeRF 360-style scenes, plus training cost.
papers.hardware: GPU model NR in the coded evidence; 27–32 min on TensoIR scenes and 55–64 min on Mip-NeRF 360 scenes are reported.
papers.limitations: The indirect-light representation is approximate and factorization remains sensitive to inaccurate geometry and priors.

papers.title: GS^3: Efficient Relighting with Triple Gaussian Splatting
papers.access: open arXiv preprint plus publisher record
papers.novelty: Uses three Gaussian families to model scene geometry, direct lighting, and indirect illumination for efficient relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: Separate splat sets encode surface, direct irradiance, and indirect transport.
papers.physical_model: PBR surface shading with explicit direct/indirect lighting decomposition.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB imagery with calibrated poses.
papers.evaluation: Novel views, relighting, intrinsic factors, training time and interactive FPS.
papers.hardware: Dual AMD EPYC CPUs, 768 GB system RAM, and NVIDIA RTX 4090; 40–70 min training and over 90 FPS are reported.
papers.limitations: Multiple splat sets increase storage/optimization complexity and learned transport may not generalize to large geometry changes.

papers.title: GlossyGS: Inverse Rendering of Glossy Objects with 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Targets high-frequency glossy reflections with geometry-aware normals and a physically structured appearance model while retaining rasterized speed.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians act as oriented glossy surface elements with material and lighting features.
papers.physical_model: Microfacet-inspired glossy BRDF and environment lighting with geometry constraints.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB images of glossy objects.
papers.evaluation: Geometry, NVS, material recovery and relighting on glossy-object datasets, plus direct efficiency comparison.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA V100 32 GB; about 1 h optimization and about 30 FPS. The cited prior configuration used 4×A100 for roughly 3 days.
papers.limitations: High-frequency lighting-material ambiguity remains difficult, particularly where geometry is weak or reflections dominate all diffuse cues.

papers.title: GeoSplatting: Towards Geometry Guided Gaussian Splatting for Physically-based Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Constrains splats to an optimizable mesh so mesh normals, opacity, and ray tracing stabilize material-light decomposition.
papers.gaussian_role: Surface-grounded Gaussians carry appearance while an explicit mesh supplies geometry and transport support.
papers.physical_model: Mesh-based visibility/ray tracing and PBR material-environment lighting decomposition.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Geometry, normals, materials, novel views, relighting, training time, and memory at different geometric resolutions.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA RTX 4090 24 GB; 15–20 min at 96³ resolution, while 128³ exceeds 1 h and 60 GB memory.
papers.limitations: The explicit mesh is a non-Gaussian dependency and higher geometry resolution sharply raises memory and fitting cost.

papers.title: GUS-IR: Gaussian Splatting with Unified Shading for Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Unifies forward and deferred shading so rough and glossy materials can share one Gaussian inverse-rendering formulation.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry geometry and intrinsic attributes; forward/deferred branches share the representation.
papers.physical_model: PBR shading with material and illumination decomposition, combining primitive- and pixel-space evaluation.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: NVS, normals, albedo/roughness, relighting, and ablations across scenes with mixed material types.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Unified shading still uses approximate visibility/global transport and inherits ambiguity from single-light RGB capture.

papers.title: IRGS: Inter-Reflective Gaussian Splatting with 2D Gaussian Ray Tracing
papers.novelty: Adds differentiable 2D-Gaussian ray tracing to inverse rendering so inter-reflection contributes to recovered materials and relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: 2D Gaussian surfels encode surface/materials and are also the ray-traced transport primitives.
papers.physical_model: Rendering equation with direct lighting, visibility, and inter-reflection estimated by Gaussian ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: NVS, geometry/material estimation, relighting and inter-reflection ablations.
papers.hardware: NVIDIA RTX 3090; about 40 min optimization and roughly 1 s per 256-ray rendered image are reported.
papers.limitations: The reported ray-traced evaluation is not real time and finite samples/geometry errors affect secondary transport.

papers.title: Reflective Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Combines split-sum deferred PBR with Gaussian-grounded inter-reflection and material-aware normal propagation for reflective scenes.
papers.gaussian_role: 2D Gaussian primitives provide surface geometry, per-pixel PBR attributes, and inter-reflection support.
papers.physical_model: Microfacet split-sum shading plus learned/transported inter-reflection.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Reflective and non-reflective NVS, geometry/material recovery, relighting, editing and efficiency.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: The split-sum and Gaussian inter-reflection modules approximate full spectral multi-bounce transport.

papers.title: SVG-IR: Spatially-Varying Gaussian Splatting for Inverse Rendering
papers.novelty: Lets one Gaussian carry spatially varying normal/material parameters and adds physically based indirect lighting for finer decomposition.
papers.gaussian_role: Spatially varying Gaussians act like compact surface patches with per-fragment attributes.
papers.physical_model: PBR direct and indirect illumination with a splat-to-fragment shading scheme.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: NVS, inverse-rendering factors, relighting, PSNR improvements and real-time rendering claims.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: More expressive per-Gaussian parameters raise model complexity, while indirect illumination is still an approximation.

papers.title: TransparentGS: Fast Inverse Rendering of Transparent Objects with Gaussians
papers.access: open arXiv preprint plus publisher record
papers.novelty: Optimizes transparent-object shape and optical parameters with Gaussian primitives and differentiable refraction/reflection rendering.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians parameterize transparent surface geometry and optical material attributes.
papers.physical_model: Geometric optics with refraction, reflection, Fresnel behavior, and differentiable ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view RGB images of transparent objects, with method-specific masks/background assumptions.
papers.evaluation: Transparent-object geometry, NVS and optical rendering on synthetic and real captures, plus fitting/render speed.
papers.hardware: GPU model NR in the coded evidence; about 1 h fitting and 31–51 FPS are reported.
papers.limitations: Transparent inverse rendering remains highly ambiguous; assumptions on interfaces/backgrounds limit general nested or scattering media.

papers.title: RTR-GS: 3D Gaussian Splatting for Inverse Rendering with Radiance Transfer and Reflection
papers.access: open arXiv abstract/preprint plus paywalled ACM venue version
papers.novelty: Combines forward radiance transfer with deferred reflection, then adds a PBR branch to separate BRDF and lighting for arbitrary reflectance.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians support a hybrid forward/deferred scene representation and carry recovered physical attributes.
papers.physical_model: Radiance transfer, deferred reflection, and physically based BRDF-light decomposition.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB images.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports NVS, normal, decomposition, relighting, and efficiency comparisons.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding: full protocol and hardware were not inspected, so no detailed performance claims are carried forward.

papers.title: GOGS: High-Fidelity Geometry and Relighting for Glossy Objects via Gaussian Surfels
papers.novelty: Uses Gaussian surfels, foundation-model geometry priors, Monte Carlo rendering-equation sampling, and 2D-Gaussian ray tracing for glossy inverse rendering.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussian surfels are the surface, material, and ray-tracing representation.
papers.physical_model: Split-sum and Monte Carlo rendering-equation evaluation with indirect illumination and anisotropic specular modeling.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB images of glossy objects.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports geometry, material separation, NVS, and relighting comparisons.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; hardware, exact capture conditions, and quantitative protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: GAINS: Gaussian-based Inverse Rendering from Sparse Multi-View Captures
papers.novelty: Regularizes sparse-view Gaussian inverse rendering with depth, normal, segmentation, intrinsic-decomposition, and diffusion priors.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians hold geometry and PBR factors stabilized by external foundation-model priors.
papers.physical_model: PBR material-light decomposition constrained by learned monocular and diffusion priors.
papers.inputs: Sparse calibrated captures ranging from 4 to 32 cameras.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports material error, relighting, and novel-view comparisons on synthetic and real objects.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; physical identifiability depends strongly on learned priors and exact hardware/protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: PhyGaP: Physically-Grounded Gaussians with Polarization Cues
papers.novelty: Adds polarimetric deferred rendering and a self-occlusion-aware environment map to reduce reflection/material ambiguity.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry geometry, normals, and reflection/material attributes estimated from RGB-polarization cues.
papers.physical_model: Polarization by reflection, PBR decomposition, and occlusion-aware incident lighting.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB with full or partial polarization measurements.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports appearance, normal, inverse-rendering and relighting results on synthetic and real scenes.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; specialized polarization capture is required and exact runtime/hardware were not inspected.

papers.title: SSD-GS: Scattering and Shadow Decomposition for Relightable 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Decomposes diffuse, anisotropic specular, shadow, and dipole-based subsurface scattering components for interpretable relighting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry decomposed surface and scattering attributes used by a progressive PBR optimizer.
papers.physical_model: Fresnel-aware anisotropic reflectance, visibility/shadow modeling, and dipole subsurface scattering.
papers.inputs: Multi-view multi-light/OLAT imagery.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports novel-light relighting and component ablations on an OLAT benchmark.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; full transport assumptions, quantitative protocol, and hardware were not inspected.

papers.title: Mirror-3DGS: Incorporating Mirror Reflections into 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Adds explicit mirror attributes and plane-mirror virtual viewpoints to reconstruct physically coherent reflections in real time.
papers.gaussian_role: Separate real and mirrored Gaussian renderings are coupled by an estimated mirror plane.
papers.physical_model: Planar mirror imaging and reflected camera geometry.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images of scenes with planar mirrors.
papers.evaluation: Mirror-region and full-image NVS on synthetic and real scenes, including real-time rendering comparisons.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Assumes planar specular mirrors and does not model rough, curved, refractive, or multi-bounce reflective interfaces.

papers.title: MirrorGaussian: Reflecting 3D Gaussians for Reconstructing Mirror Reflections
papers.novelty: Jointly optimizes a mirror plane and dual real/virtual Gaussian renderings derived from mirror symmetry.
papers.gaussian_role: The same Gaussian scene is reflected across an estimated plane and composited with the direct rendering.
papers.physical_model: Planar mirror symmetry and dual-view rasterization.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images with mirror regions.
papers.evaluation: Synthetic and real mirror-scene NVS, mirror-plane/region ablations, editing, and real-time rendering.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Limited to planar mirror geometry; cannot explain rough reflection, transmission, curved mirrors, or general secondary transport.

papers.title: RefGaussian: Disentangling Reflections from 3D Gaussian Splatting for Realistic Rendering
papers.novelty: Separates transmitted and reflected radiance into two spherical-harmonic components with local regularization to prevent reflected content becoming false geometry.
papers.gaussian_role: Each Gaussian carries separate transmitted and reflected appearance components.
papers.physical_model: Additive reflection/transmission decomposition with view-dependent spherical harmonics; no explicit BRDF or ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view RGB images containing total and semi-reflections.
papers.evaluation: Novel-view appearance, depth, component separation, scene editing, PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: The component split is regularized rather than physically identified and cannot model refraction, Fresnel coupling, or multi-bounce transport.

papers.title: 3D Gaussian Ray Tracing: Fast Tracing of Particle Scenes
papers.novelty: Develops acceleration structures and analytic intersection/sampling machinery for tracing Gaussian particle scenes instead of only rasterizing them.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are volumetric particles directly intersected and accumulated along rays.
papers.physical_model: Ray-based transmittance and radiance accumulation through anisotropic Gaussian density.
papers.inputs: Preconstructed Gaussian scenes and ray queries.
papers.evaluation: Rendering correctness, ray-query cost and application-specific performance against rasterization/tracing alternatives.
papers.hardware: The primary paper reports representative rates of 78, 190, and 119 FPS for evaluated rendering configurations; the GPU model was not recovered in this coding pass.
papers.limitations: It is principally a renderer, not a method for identifying material or lighting physics; rates are workload-specific.

papers.title: TranSplat: Instant Object Relighting in Gaussian Splatting via Spherical Harmonic Radiance Transfer
papers.novelty: Stores spherical-harmonic radiance-transfer coefficients on splats for instantaneous relighting under novel environment illumination.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry object appearance and compact precomputed transport coefficients.
papers.physical_model: Spherical-harmonic precomputed radiance transfer.
papers.inputs: A reconstructed Gaussian object and environment illumination.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports relighting fidelity and interactive performance.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; full transport bandwidth, editability constraints, hardware and quantitative protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: RT-GS: Gaussian Splatting with Reflection and Transmittance Primitives
papers.novelty: Uses separate Gaussian primitive sets for reflected and transmitted appearance, coupled through microfacet shading and differentiable ray tracing.
papers.gaussian_role: Reflection and transmittance Gaussians explicitly represent the two optical paths.
papers.physical_model: Microfacet reflection, transmittance, and differentiable ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB observations of semi-transparent specular scenes.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports qualitative and quantitative NVS on scenes where reflection and through-surface content coexist.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; exact interface assumptions, hardware, and generality to nested refractive/scattering media were not inspected.

papers.title: RT-Splatting: Joint Reflection-Transmission Modeling with Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Factors geometric occupancy from optical opacity and combines surface reflection with volume transmission in one Gaussian representation.
papers.gaussian_role: One Gaussian set is interpreted both as a surface for high-frequency reflection and a volume for transmission.
papers.physical_model: Hybrid surface-volume optics with reflection/transmission decomposition and specular-aware gradient gating.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB images of semi-transparent, specular scenes.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports reflection/transmission quality, NVS, real-time rendering and editing.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; the exact physical interface model and hardware were not inspected, and gradient gating is an optimization prior rather than a conservation law.

papers.title: RefracGS: Novel View Synthesis Through Refractive Water Surfaces with 3D Gaussian Ray Tracing
papers.novelty: Models camera rays refracted by a water interface before tracing a Gaussian scene, targeting views observed through moving or shaped water surfaces.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians represent the submerged scene and are queried by refracted rays.
papers.physical_model: Geometric refraction through a water-air interface and Gaussian ray tracing.
papers.inputs: Multi-view imagery observed through refractive water surfaces.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports NVS comparisons on refractive-water datasets.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; interface reconstruction, caustics, participating-media scattering, exact runtime and hardware were not inspected.

papers.title: StochasticSplats: Stochastic Rasterization for Sorting-Free 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Replaces depth sorting with an unbiased Monte Carlo estimator of the volume-rendering equation and exposes a sample-count quality/cost control.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are participating volume primitives sampled stochastically in an OpenGL renderer.
papers.physical_model: Monte Carlo estimation of the volume-rendering equation and transmittance through overlapping Gaussians.
papers.inputs: Existing Gaussian scenes plus a user-selected sample count.
papers.evaluation: Appearance error/artifacts, rendering cost versus sample count, and comparison with sorted rasterization.
papers.hardware: Implemented with OpenGL shaders; at a stated reasonable quality the paper reports more than 4× the speed of sorted rasterization, but no absolute hardware-normalized rate is coded.
papers.limitations: It improves numerical rendering consistency rather than estimating real scene physics; stochastic noise and workload-dependent convergence remain.

papers.title: WaterSplatting: Fast Underwater 3D Scene Reconstruction Using Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Embeds a depth-dependent underwater image-formation model in 3DGS to separate scene radiance from attenuation and backscatter.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians represent scene geometry/radiance while global or spatial medium parameters explain water degradation.
papers.physical_model: Underwater formation with wavelength-dependent attenuation and backscatter.
papers.inputs: Posed underwater RGB images.
papers.evaluation: Underwater NVS and restoration on synthetic and real sequences using PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS and qualitative color/depth analyses.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Assumes a simplified medium model and calibrated poses; spatially heterogeneous water, caustics, and dynamic particles are weakly covered.

papers.title: SeaSplat: Representing Underwater Scenes with 3D Gaussian Splatting and a Physically Grounded Image Formation Model
papers.novelty: Jointly reconstructs scene Gaussians and a physically grounded underwater attenuation/backscatter field instead of baking haze into color.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians carry clear-scene radiance and geometry; an explicit water model maps them to observed pixels.
papers.physical_model: Depth- and wavelength-dependent direct transmission plus veiling/backscatter.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view underwater RGB images.
papers.evaluation: NVS, recovered clear appearance, depth/medium ablations and comparison across synthetic and real underwater scenes.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: The reduced underwater model cannot fully capture multiple scattering, dynamic illumination, particulate transients, or severe pose uncertainty.

papers.title: DehazeGS: Seeing Through Fog with 3D Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Integrates atmospheric scattering into Gaussian rendering so scene radiance and haze can be optimized jointly from foggy multi-view images.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians encode clear-scene geometry/radiance and are rendered through an estimated haze field.
papers.physical_model: Atmospheric scattering with transmission and airlight/backscatter terms.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view images captured in fog or haze.
papers.evaluation: Dehazed NVS, depth/geometry, synthetic parameter recovery and real-scene appearance comparisons.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: Homogeneous or low-dimensional haze assumptions do not capture turbulent, spatially varying, or multiple-scattering fog.

papers.title: Radiative Gaussian Splatting for Efficient X-ray Novel View Synthesis
papers.novelty: Reformulates splatting for X-ray radiative projection, replacing opaque alpha compositing with attenuation-consistent line integration.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians parameterize volumetric attenuation and are analytically projected into X-ray images.
papers.physical_model: Beer–Lambert attenuation along X-ray paths.
papers.inputs: Calibrated multi-view X-ray projections.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports X-ray NVS quality and efficiency versus radiance-field baselines.
papers.hardware: X-ray projection imagery is the required sensor setup; exact scanner/GPU details were not coded from full text.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; it targets projection synthesis rather than a general diagnostic CT reconstruction guarantee, and full hardware/protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: R²-Gaussian: Rectifying Radiative Gaussian Splatting for Tomographic Reconstruction
papers.novelty: Rectifies radiative Gaussian projection and optimization for sparse-view tomographic reconstruction rather than only X-ray view synthesis.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are the reconstructable volumetric attenuation basis.
papers.physical_model: Beer–Lambert/X-ray transform with differentiable Gaussian line integrals.
papers.inputs: Sparse calibrated X-ray or CT projections.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports tomographic reconstruction fidelity under projection sparsity and efficiency comparisons.
papers.hardware: Tomographic projection data are the required sensor setup; scanner and GPU details were not coded from full text.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; clinical validity, uncertainty, hardware and full acquisition protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: Thermal3D-GS: Physics-induced 3D Gaussians for Thermal Infrared Novel-view Synthesis
papers.novelty: Introduces thermal-physics-informed Gaussian attributes and RGB–thermal coupling for infrared novel-view synthesis.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians jointly encode geometry and modality-specific visible/thermal radiance.
papers.physical_model: Thermal emission/appearance constraints used to regularize cross-modal Gaussian reconstruction.
papers.inputs: Calibrated RGB and thermal infrared multi-view images.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports thermal NVS and cross-modal reconstruction comparisons.
papers.hardware: A paired or registered RGB–thermal capture setup is required; exact camera models and compute were not coded from full text.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; the extent of explicit heat/radiative transport versus learned thermal appearance and hardware details were not fully inspected.

papers.title: GS-DOT: Gaussian splatting-based image reconstruction for diffuse optical tomography
papers.novelty: Represents absorption as sparse anisotropic Gaussians and differentiates through photon-diffusion response functions for diffuse optical tomography.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are the unknown absorption-coefficient field optimized against time-resolved optical measurements.
papers.physical_model: Photon diffusion in highly scattering media, replacing ray transport with diffusion Green functions.
papers.inputs: Time-resolved source-detector point-spread functions.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports localization, absorption quantification, noise robustness and memory reduction on synthetic tissue models.
papers.hardware: A time-resolved diffuse-optical measurement geometry is assumed; exact instrumentation and compute were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; validation is synthetic, the diffusion approximation has regime limits, and full runtime/hardware are unavailable.

papers.title: Inverse Radiative Transport for Infrared Scenes with Gaussian Primitives
papers.access: paywalled ACM record plus open author manuscript
papers.novelty: Solves inverse long-wave infrared transport on 2D Gaussian surfels, separating self-emission from reflected thermal radiation and enabling re-heating interventions.
papers.gaussian_role: 2D Gaussians represent surfaces and carry temperature, emissivity, roughness, diffuse coefficient, and a spherical-Gaussian radiance cache.
papers.physical_model: Stefan–Boltzmann/Kirchhoff gray-body emission and reflection, radiosity, hemicube visibility, and spherical-Gaussian outgoing radiance.
papers.inputs: 300 paired RGB–thermal images across three radiator temperatures in the HiGS dataset, plus synthetic scenes.
papers.evaluation: Synthetic and real thermal rendering, reflection/emission separation, material/temperature recovery, and moved/reheated source tests.
papers.hardware: Paired RGB and thermal cameras plus a controllable radiator are reported; compute hardware was not recovered in the inspected evidence.
papers.limitations: Assumes steady state, constant emissivity across the LWIR band, gray-body behavior, and simplified surface reflection; conduction/convection are out of scope.

papers.title: Dynamic Global Illumination for Interactive Gaussian Splatting Scenes in Real Time
papers.publication: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics 2026; DOI 10.1109/TVCG.2026.3689199
papers.access: paywalled IEEE publisher record; no open preprint recovered
papers.novelty: Builds a real-time global-illumination pipeline for mixed Gaussian/mesh scenes using compound stochastic ray tracing and a hardware Gaussian rasterizer.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are editable surface-light-transport primitives that coexist with conventional meshes.
papers.physical_model: Multi-bounce diffuse and one-bounce glossy global illumination for area, directional, and environment lights.
papers.inputs: Interactive 3D Gaussian and hybrid mesh/Gaussian scenes with editable materials and lights.
papers.evaluation: The publisher abstract reports real-time rendering across interactive/dynamic scene configurations.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 publisher-only coding; exact hardware, rates, image-quality protocol and approximation error were not accessible.

papers.title: Efficient Object Reconstruction with Differentiable Area Light Shading
papers.access: paywalled ACM record plus open author manuscript
papers.novelty: Uses active area lighting and differentiable linearly transformed cosines so Gaussian inverse rendering recovers roughness with fewer photographs.
papers.gaussian_role: A 3D Gaussian object pipeline is one of the two tested differentiable surface representations carrying recovered BRDF/geometry.
papers.physical_model: Area-light rendering, BRDF estimation, shadow visibility weighting, and linearly transformed cosines.
papers.inputs: Controlled photographs under active area or point lighting.
papers.evaluation: BRDF/material error, relighting PSNR, photo count and optimization time; area light gives about +3 dB or one-fifth the photos, and LTC improves optimization time by 25%.
papers.hardware: Controlled area-light capture is reported; compute hardware was not recovered in this coding pass.
papers.limitations: Requires active calibrated lighting and the paper evaluates both mesh and Gaussian pipelines, so some conclusions are representation-independent.

papers.title: Arbitrary Optics for Gaussian Splatting Using Space Warping
papers.novelty: Warps the Gaussian scene differentiably to support highly distorted fisheye and other non-pinhole optics without pre-undistortion.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are transformed by a calibrated differentiable optical warping function before rasterization.
papers.physical_model: General camera projection/distortion expressed as differentiable space warping.
papers.inputs: Multi-view images acquired with arbitrary/fisheye optics and camera calibration.
papers.evaluation: Synthetic and real distorted-image reconstruction, NVS appearance, and geometric behavior.
papers.hardware: Fisheye/high-distortion camera optics are explicitly evaluated; exact compute setup was not coded.
papers.limitations: Targets geometric optics/projection rather than diffraction, aberration PSFs, exposure, sensor noise, or unknown-lens joint calibration.

papers.title: HDRGS: High Dynamic Range Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Reconstructs HDR Gaussian radiance from multi-exposure LDR views using luminance-aware features, an asymmetric tone-mapping grid, and coarse-to-fine fitting.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians represent HDR scene radiance; a learned camera/tone-mapping module maps irradiance to LDR pixels.
papers.physical_model: Exposure-dependent radiometric formation and learned camera response/tone mapping.
papers.inputs: Unstructured multi-view LDR images with varying exposures.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports HDR reconstruction and NVS on synthetic and real scenes, including convergence analyses.
papers.hardware: A multi-exposure LDR capture setup is required; exact GPU and timing were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; learned tone mapping may not identify a transferable physical camera response and full hardware/protocol were not inspected.

papers.title: Cinematic Gaussians: Real-Time HDR Radiance Fields with Depth of Field
papers.novelty: Jointly reconstructs HDR radiance and controllable depth of field using analytical Gaussian convolution under a thin-lens camera model.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians encode HDR radiance and admit analytical aperture/defocus convolution.
papers.physical_model: Thin-lens depth of field, exposure variation, and tone mapping.
papers.inputs: Multi-view LDR images with varying exposure, aperture, and focus distance.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports HDR/defocus reconstruction, refocusing, NVS, and real-time cinematic rendering.
papers.hardware: Capture varies exposure time, aperture and focus distance; exact camera/GPU details were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; the thin-lens model omits complex aberrations, diffraction, rolling shutter and sensor noise.

papers.title: Gaussian Splatting on the Move: Blur and Rolling Shutter Compensation for Natural Camera Motion
papers.novelty: Differentiably integrates time-varying camera pose during exposure to model motion blur and rolling shutter, initialized by visual-inertial odometry.
papers.gaussian_role: A static Gaussian scene is rendered repeatedly/approximately along a continuous exposure-time camera trajectory.
papers.physical_model: Temporal exposure integration, rolling-shutter row timing, and continuous camera motion.
papers.inputs: Handheld video with motion blur and rolling shutter plus VIO velocity/pose estimates.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports synthetic and real reconstruction/NVS under natural camera motion.
papers.hardware: A handheld rolling-shutter camera and visual-inertial odometry are used; exact compute hardware was not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; assumes a mostly static scene and adequate VIO, and does not model full sensor response or independently moving objects.

papers.title: Every Camera Effect, Every Time, All at Once: 4D Gaussian Ray Tracing for Physics-based Camera Effect Data Generation
papers.novelty: Ray traces reconstructed 4D Gaussian scenes to generate controllable dynamic videos with fisheye, rolling-shutter, and other physical camera effects.
papers.gaussian_role: 4D Gaussians encode dynamic geometry/radiance and are the scene queried by an effect-aware ray tracer.
papers.physical_model: Physics-based camera rays and time sampling for lens distortion, rolling shutter, motion/defocus effects.
papers.inputs: Multi-view videos used to reconstruct a dynamic scene, followed by user-specified virtual camera effects.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports quality/speed and an eight-scene benchmark spanning four camera effects.
papers.hardware: NR in inspected primary evidence.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; it generates training data rather than identifying real camera parameters, and exact hardware/effect fidelity were not inspected.

papers.title: E2GS: Event Enhanced Gaussian Splatting
papers.novelty: Fuses event measurements with blurred frames to supervise Gaussian reconstruction and deblur fast-motion imagery.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians represent scene radiance; an event/frame observation model supplies complementary supervision.
papers.physical_model: Event-camera brightness-change measurements coupled to blurred frame formation.
papers.inputs: Synchronized event streams and blurry intensity images.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports synthetic/real deblurring, NVS, training efficiency, and 140 FPS rendering.
papers.hardware: A frame-plus-event camera setup is required; 140 FPS is reported, but the GPU model was not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; relies on synchronized hybrid sensing and event contrast/noise calibration; full compute details were not inspected.

papers.title: Ev-GS: Event-based Gaussian splatting for Efficient and Accurate Radiance Field Rendering
papers.novelty: Infers a Gaussian radiance field from a monocular event camera with pure event supervision, targeting fast motion and low light.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are optimized from accumulated brightness-change events rather than conventional frames.
papers.physical_model: Event-generation response based on log-intensity changes.
papers.inputs: Monocular asynchronous event streams and camera poses.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports NVS quality, blur reduction, reconstruction efficiency, and computing occupancy.
papers.hardware: A monocular event camera is required; exact sensor and GPU models were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; event thresholds/noise and pose estimation can bias reconstruction, and absolute radiance is weakly observed.

papers.title: EventSplat: 3D Gaussian Splatting from Moving Event Cameras for Real-time Rendering
papers.novelty: Initializes Gaussian reconstruction with an event-to-video prior and fits spline camera trajectories for fast moving event cameras.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians encode scene radiance reconstructed from asynchronous events and prior-generated intensity cues.
papers.physical_model: Event response plus continuous-time spline camera motion.
papers.inputs: Moving event-camera streams and estimated poses.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports NVS fidelity and order-of-magnitude rendering speed over event-NeRF approaches.
papers.hardware: A moving event camera is required; exact sensor/GPU details and absolute render rates were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; depends on an event-to-video prior that can inject appearance bias and on reliable trajectory estimation.

papers.title: SweepEvGS: Event-Based 3D Gaussian Splatting for Macro and Micro Radiance Field Rendering from a Single Sweep
papers.novelty: Reconstructs macro- and micro-scale Gaussian radiance fields from one continuous event-camera sweep.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are optimized from high-temporal-resolution event observations collected along a single trajectory.
papers.physical_model: Event brightness-change formation and continuous camera sweep geometry.
papers.inputs: A single calibrated moving event-camera sweep.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports rendering quality and efficiency across macro/micro scenes.
papers.hardware: A moving event-camera capture is required; exact sensor and compute setup were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; coverage from one sweep and event calibration bound geometry/appearance completeness.

papers.title: PolarGS: Polarimetric Cues for Ambiguity-Free Gaussian Splatting with Accurate Geometry Recovery
papers.novelty: Uses degree and angle of linear polarization to correct reflective photometry and densify textureless geometry.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians are color-corrected and densified using polarimetric orientation cues.
papers.physical_model: Polarization by reflection encoded through DoLP/AoLP constraints and polarization-guided PatchMatch.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB and polarization measurements.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports geometry and appearance improvements in reflective and textureless regions.
papers.hardware: A polarization-capable camera/capture is required; exact sensor and compute models were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; polarization ambiguities, partial polarization and specialized sensing constrain general use.

papers.title: Flash-Splat: 3D Reflection Removal with Flash Cues and Gaussian Splats
papers.novelty: Uses unpaired flash/no-flash observations and Gaussian novel-view consistency to separate transmitted and reflected 3D scenes.
papers.gaussian_role: Separate Gaussian representations reconstruct transmitted and reflected scene components.
papers.physical_model: Illumination intervention through flash/no-flash reflection separation.
papers.inputs: Multi-view RGB images with unpaired flash and ambient illumination states.
papers.evaluation: The primary abstract reports real-world transmitted/reflected reconstruction and comparison with reflection-separation baselines.
papers.hardware: A controllable on-camera or calibrated flash capture is required; exact camera/GPU details were not inspected.
papers.limitations: E1 coding; requires active illumination changes and does not recover a general BRDF, refraction, or full multi-bounce transport.

papers.title: 3DGUT: Enabling Distorted Cameras and Secondary Rays in Gaussian Splatting
papers.access: open arXiv full text; venue version may be paywalled
papers.novelty: Uses an unscented-transform projection for nonlinear cameras and a ray-tracing-compatible Gaussian formulation, enabling distorted lenses and secondary rays without reducing them to a pinhole rasterizer.
papers.gaussian_role: Gaussians remain the primary scene primitives while their projection and intersection are generalized beyond affine screen-space splatting.
papers.physical_model: Nonlinear camera projection through the unscented transform plus ray-based visibility for secondary transport paths.
papers.inputs: Posed multi-view images from pinhole or distorted camera models and Gaussian scenes used for secondary-ray evaluation.
papers.evaluation: Novel-view quality under multiple camera models, secondary-ray applications, timing, and comparisons with conventional Gaussian rasterization.
papers.hardware: Reported rendering configurations take about 3.77–4.98 ms, corresponding to roughly 200–277 FPS; device identity is workload-specific in the inspected evidence.
papers.limitations: The generalized renderer does not by itself identify a camera, material, or complete global-illumination solution; acceleration quality and scene settings qualify the timing.
